title: Russian Primary Chronicle: The Calling of the Varangians
description: The foundation myth of Russia: the Slavic tribes invite the Varangian Rus to come and rule over them.
introduction: The Primary Chronicle (Tale of Bygone Years) is the foundational text of Russian, Ukrainian, and Belarusian historiography. Compiled by the monk Nestor at the Caves Monastery in Kiev around 1113, it traces the origin of the Slavic peoples and records the famous 'invitation' of 862 — when the quarreling Slavic and Finnic tribes supposedly asked the Varangian Rus to come establish order.

translation.original_text: Се повести времяньных лет, откуду есть пошла Руская земля.
translation.text: These are the stories of bygone years, whence the Russian land had its beginning.

translation.original_text: Земля наша велика и обильна, а наряда в ней нет. Да пойдете княжить и владети нами.
translation.text: Our land is great and rich, but there is no order in it. Come and rule over us.
